texto: Fear is like a weed in a garden. Once you allow it to take root, it spreads, replicating itself, until, eventually, it chokes out all the other life there. Trust, love, kindness – none of those things can ever really bloom in a garden of fear.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Breeana Puttroff: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “Fear is like a weed in a garden. Once you allow it to take root, it spreads, replicating itself, …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que motivacao, fear, inspirational-quote pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de inspiracao e inspirational-quote.

frase_original: Fear is like a weed in a garden. Once you allow it to take root, it spreads, replicating itself, until, eventually, it chokes out all the other life there. Trust, love, kindness – none of those things can ever really bloom in a garden of fear.

texto: He shook his head, trying to shake the convoluted thoughts loose. He couldn't worry about it right now. Not about how Thomas felt, or how Quinn did, or even how he, himself did. They were where they were, and there were things to do. First and foremost, there was a baby to deliver.
explicacao: Citação de Breeana Puttroff sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “He shook his head, trying to shake the convoluted thoughts loose. He couldn't worry about it righ…” articula amor, baby, romance em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como romance.

frase_original: He shook his head, trying to shake the convoluted thoughts loose. He couldn't worry about it right now. Not about how Thomas felt, or how Quinn did, or even how he, himself did. They were where they were, and there were things to do. First and foremost, there was a baby to deliver.
